(His life did not fulfil its promise). be-vafā , adj False; faithless; treacherous. Nauj tum par kisī kā jī nikle! Tum baṛe be-vafā ajī nikle! Wom. Jān Sāhab. May never heart be set on you! You are so faithless and untrue! be-vafāī , n. f. Faithlessness; inconstancy.

def: pron. That, the; that person; that thing; — he, she, it; — (pl.) those; they (i.q. we); — (by wǒh, 'he,' a wife refers to her husband, it being considered disrespectful to take his name)
more: pron. 1. He; she; it; that. 2. ( ve ) They. Gālib vazīfah-khār ho, do shāh ko duā! Voh din gaë ki kahte the naukar nahīṅ hūṅ maiṅ . Gālib. Gālib! a pensioner you are, may Heaven bless the king! Gone are the days when you were wont 'No servant I' to sing. 3. By ' voh' he, a wife refers to her husband as a mark of respect, it being considered disrespectful to take his name. 1. Ḍolī ke sāth lāl kue se na āë voh, Bī, kaise ḍāṅvāṅ-ḍol phire haiṅ kahār āj! Wom. Rāhat. 2. Prītam aisī prīt kar jaise soṭā bhaṅg, Voh toṛe vā-kī pāṅsrī vah lipṭe vā-ke aṅg . Your love should be as hempen leaf towards the pestle shows, She clasps his body closer while he breaks her ribs with blows. voh jo . That which; who; what. voh shakhs jis par ḍigrī ho . G. G. The person against whom a decree is issued.
pron. That, he, she, it, the; (occasionally like وي) they, those.
pron. plur. (dakh.) for وي we, They, those.

more: adj. 1. One (ايک 1). 2. God. Uskī zāt vahdahū lā-sharīk hai . God is one and indivisible. 3. ( Gram .) Singular ( ek-bachan ). vāhid-ul-ain , adj. One-eyed. vāhid shāhid . God is my witness. vāhid shāhid ; Illit. vād shād , adj. Acquainted with; cognizant. Maiṅ to un se vād shād bhī nahīṅ .
(part. act. of وحد Heb. ) adj. One, sole, individual, single, singular. واحد شاهد wāḥid shāhid, 1. God is my witness. 2. ( met. ) Giving, bestowing.
subs: God (is my) witness; — (met.) adj. Giving, bestowing; — acting kindly (towards, -se), treating kindly; — acquainted (with), cognizant (of)
(-se), To pursue a friendly course of conduct (towards), to treat (one) kindly; — to have knowledge (of), be acquainted (with)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَاحِد (wāhid), from Arabic وَاحِد (wāḥid).

ety: v.n. fr. وحد 'to be single, or alone,' &c.
def: adj. Alone, unique, single, singular; — sole, separated; — s.m. Sole one; unique one, incomparable one
more: (from وحد) adj. Alone, unique, singular. وحيد العصر waḥīdu-l-aﻌṣr, The nonpareil of the age.
subs: s.m. The nonpareil of the age.

ety: for A. واحدية, abst. s. fr. wāḥidī, rel. n. fr. wāḥid
def: s.f. Unity; — unitarianism; — name of a Muḥammadan sect.

ety: act. part. of وهم 'to think, imagine,' &c.
def: part. adj. Thinking, imagining, fancying, being of opinion.
more: part. act. (of وهم) Thinking, imagining, being of opinion.

ety: for A. واهمة wāhimat, fem. of wāhim
def: s.m. Imagination, fancy.
more: (part. act. fem. of وهم) s. m. Imagination, fancy.

def: adv. Just there; on the spot.
more: adv. Just at that time; as soon as.

def: adv. That very place; in that same or very place, just there; — that very time, that same or very instant, just then, precisely at that moment; immediately, at once; forthwith; — in that very manner, just so. even so.
more: adv. That very (time, place, &c.), immediately, exactly there.

def: s.f. An army, body of forces; a division of an army (consisting of 81 elephants, 81 cars, 243 horse, 405 foot); a battalion; a cohort; — a river
subs: s.m. Lord or leader of an army, a general, a commanding officer; — ʻlord of rivers,' an epithet of the ocean.

def: adj. & adv. = vahis, q.v. adj. & adv. Out, without, outwards, outside, apart from; outer, exterior, external; foreign (see bahir)
subs: adj. (f. -ā), Being out, being outside, exterior, external; — expelled; excluded; — expired, elapsed (as a period of time); — irrelevant; inattentive, careless
s.m. A foreign or remote country; a place abroad; a place without a town or village; the outskirts of a town or village. (For other compounds, see bahir.)

ety: changeable to vahir, vahih̥, vahiś, or vahish, according to the nature of the initial letter of the word to which it is prefixed
def: adj. & adv. Out, without, outwards, outside, apart from; outer, exterior, external; foreign (see bahir)
subs: adj. (f. -ā), Being out, being outside, exterior, external; — expelled; excluded; — expired, elapsed (as a period of time); — irrelevant; inattentive, careless
s.m. A foreign or remote country; a place abroad; a place without a town or village; the outskirts of a town or village. (For other compounds, see bahir.)

def: s.f. pl. (of wāhī, or of wāhiyat, fem. of wāhī), Absurdities; absurdity, nonsense, fiddle-faddle; — obscenity.

ety: prob. Prk. वहडओ or वहल्लओ; S. वह (rt. वह्)+ल+कः
def: s.m. Onset, attack, assault.
s. m. Attack, onset.
n. m. 1. Fancy; imagination (خيال). 2. Doubt; suspicion. 3. A superstition.

ety: inf. n. of وهم 'to think, imagine,' &c.
def: s.m. Thinking, imagining, conceiving (esp. a false idea); — opinion, conjecture; imagination, idea, fancy; — suspicion, doubt; scruple, caution; distrust, anxiety, apprehension, fear; — a superstition; — (for quwwat-ě-wahm) the intellectual faculty, power of imagination
more: s. m. Imagination, idea, fancy, conjecture, opinion, anxiety, apprehension.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَهْم (wahm), borrowed from Arabic وَهْم (wahm), from وَهَمَ (wahama). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi وہم (vhm /⁠vaham⁠/).

ety: for A. وهمة wahmat, n. of un. fr. wahm
def: s.m. A fancy, an idea; a whim.
adj. Imaginary, conjectural, fanciful, ideal, visionary; — suspicious, distrustful, apprehensive; — superstitious; — hypochondriacal.
more: adj. Imaginary, conjectural, visionary, fanciful, ideal.

def: (for A. وهمية wahmīyat, fem. of wahmī), adj. f. = wahmī, q.v. adj. Imaginary, conjectural, fanciful, ideal, visionary; — suspicious, distrustful, apprehensive; — superstitious; — hypochondriacal.

s.m. Fire; an epithet of Agni, the god of fire; — the fire of the stomach, the digestive faculty, gastric fluid; — digestion; appetite; — leadwort, Plumbago zeylanica; — the marking-nut plant; — a symbolical expression for the number three.
adv. = bahori = bahuri, q.v. adv. Again, a second time.
adj. A dialec. (or corr.) form of bahut, q.v.
s.m. A wild animal; a fierce, shy, or untamed animal.

s.f. A desert, solitude, dreary place; — loneliness, solitariness, dreariness; — sadness, grief, care; — wildness, fierceness, ferocity, savageness; barbarity, barbarism; — timidity, fear, fright, dread, terror, horror; — distraction, madness
more: n. f. 1. Wildness; barbarity; barbarism. 2. Dreariness. 3. Horror. 4. Madness. vahshat-asar, vahshat-angez, vahshat-nāk , Desolate; horrible; harrowing; bewildering. vahshat-zadah , adv. Awe-struck; aghast. vahshat h. , v. n. To be bewildered or distracted.

s. f. 1. Fright, fear, dread, horrour, loneliness, solitariness. 2. A desert, a solitude, a dreary place. 3. Wildness, savageness, ferocity. وحشت اثر waḥshat-as̤ar, Impressing with horrour, frightful. وحشت انگيز waḥshat-angez, Frightful, horrible, shocking. وحشت زده waḥshat-zada, Aghast, terrified, struck with loneliness. وحشت ناک waḥshat-nāk, Frightful.
subs: adj. Impressing with horror; horrible, frightful
or waḥshat-angez, or waḥshat-nāk, adj. Desolate, dreary, frightful, horrible, shocking, harrowing; bewildering
adj. Struck with loneliness; awe-struck, aghast, terrified
v.n. To be bewildered, to be distracted, &c.

def: adj. Wild, untamed; shy; unsociable; — uncultivated; uncivilized, barbarous; savage; untractable; fierce, ferocious; brutish; cruel; — s.m. A wild beast; a brute; a savage
more: adj. 1. Wild; barbarous; savage; ferocious. 2. Brutish; cruel. 3. Uncultivated; not civilized. vahshī , n. m. 1. A wild beast. 2. A brute; savage nīm-vahshī , adj. Half-civilized; semi-barbarous.
adj. Wild, savage, ferocious; s. A wild beast, a brute, a savage.

ety: waḥshī, q.v.+panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Vedic त्वन)+कं
def: s.m. Wildness; shyness, unsociableness; savageness; fierceness, ferocity.
adj. corr. of bahu-mitr, q.v.s.v. bahu.
adv. dialec. forms of wahīṅ, q.v.
more: adv. (dakh.) See وون woṅ or وهين wahiṅ.

def: adj. Being outside, situated without; outer, external, exterior; — not belonging to the family, or to the country; strange, foreign; — expelled from caste or society; — s.m. A stranger, a foreigner; — a man of low caste; an outcast.
intj. O! strange! ho! fy! pshaw! — (as a contrac. of wa ai) and O! — waʼe-bhaʼi, intj — wai.
more: interj. Fy! ho! &c. Also a contract. of واي wa ai, And O!

def: pron. emphat. (dialec.) = wahī (see wah).
s.f. = bāʼī, q.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = vyay, q.v.; — 3˚ prefix = be, q.v. s.f. Lady, mistress, madam; woman, wife (local; cf. bāʼīkā); — dancing-girl, harlot; mother bawd, la bonne
subs: s.f. Mother bawd, the old abbess (syn. nāyikā).

def: s.m. corr. of vaivasvat, q.v. s.m. A name of Yama (as son of Vivasvat); — the seventh Manu, the Manu of the present period (as son of Vivasvat and brother of Yama); — the planet Saturn; — name of one of the Rudras.
s.m. Superhuman power or might; power; wealth; greatness, pre-eminence, grandeur, majesty, glory, magnificence, splendour
subs: adj. Possessed of power, &c.; powerful, wealthy, great, grand, noble, magnificent, &c.

def: s.m. Predicting anything bad, threatening (with); — threat; denunciation; commination; — promising anything good.
more: (from وعد) s. 1. Threatening, or predicting any thing bad, threat, denunciation. 2. Promising any thing good.

def: s.m. The king of Videh (father of Sītā); — a dweller in Videh; — a trader by caste, a merchant; — name of one of the mixed castes (whose business is attendance in the women's apartments); — a man of that caste (the son of a Vaiśya by a Brāhman woman).
s.m. A trader or merchant by caste; — a man of a mixed caste (i.q. vaideh, q.v.).
s.f. An epithet of Sītā (daughter of Janak king of Videh); — the wife of a trader or merchant; — a woman of the Vaideh caste.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another country, foreign, strange; exotic; — a foreigner, &c. (i.q. videśī, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to another country, foreign, strange; exotic; — a foreigner, &c. (i.q. videśī, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī), Enjoined by rule; conformable to rule; prescribed, appointed, — giving rules or directions, preceptive, ritual; legal.

s.m. Name of a particular position of the sun and moon when they are on the same side of either solstice and of equal declination but of opposite direction (this is considered a malignant aspect; cf. vyatīpāt).
s.f. The yog-star of the twenty-seventh Nakshatra or lunar mansion.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Derived from, or conformable to, the Vedas; relating to the Vedas; prescribed by Vedic ritual; scriptural, sacred; — knowing the Vedas; — a Brāhmaṇ versed in the Vedas.
ex: وَیدِک زَمانے میں گوشْت کھانے کے رِواج چَلْتے تھیں
vaidik zamāne mẽ gośt khāne ke rivāj calte thẽ
Ritual of meat eating was prevailed in vedic era.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit वैदिक (vaidika).

def: s.f. A certain division of the Sanskrit grammar containing explanations of portions of the Vedas.
s.m. A gem of a bark-blue colour, lapis lazuli.

adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the Vedas; conformable to the Vedas, Vedic; conversant with the Vedas; — relating to medicine; practising medicine; medical; — a follower of the Veda; one well versed in the Vedas, a Panḍit, a learned man, doctor; — a man of the medical caste (originally the offspring of a Brāhmaṇ by a Vaiśya woman); a medical man, physician (i.q. baid; syn. ḥakīm)
subs: s.m. 'Lord of physicians'; epithet of Śiva; — and of Dhanvantarī (chief physician in the court of Indra)

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating to medicine, medical; — a physician, doctor, medical man (i.q. baid; — syn. ḥakīm).
(Braj), s.m. pl. of ويديه vaidya, q.v.
intj. Well! come! come on! away then! bravo!
s.m. A banner, flag; the banner or emblem of Indra; — the palace of Indra.

s.f. A flag, banner; an ensign; — a kind of garland; the necklace of Vishṇu (composed of five gems, viz. pearl, ruby, emerald, sapphire, and diamond); — the plant Sesbana œgyptiaca; — and Premna spinosa
subs: s.f. The necklace of Vishṇu (i.q. vaijayantī, q.v.).

def: s.m. Evening, &c. (i.q. vi-kāl, q.v.). s.m. Afternoon, close of day, twilight, evening, dusk.
adj. Belonging to, or occurring in, the afternoon or evening.

s.m. Incompleteness, imperfection; impaired state or condition; imbecility; deficiency, defect; want; incompetency, insufficiency; mutilation, defective or imperfect state of any limb or organ, lameness; — non-existence; — agitation, flurry, perplexity, consternation, confusion; surprise, astonishment, wonder.
adj. Modified, changed; — s.m. Name of a class of deities.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating to hermits, monastic; — a Brāhmaṇ of the third religious order, a hermit, an anchorite.
s.f. A vessel used for frying the meal offered in sacrifice.
s.m. A kind of gem (said to resemble a diamond or burnt diamond; — according to others, the common loadstone or magnet).
s.m. A name of Vishṇu; — and of Indra; — the paradise or heaven of Vishṇu (its site is variously described as in the northern ocean or on the eastern peak of Mount Meru; — cf. baikunṭh)
subs: s.m. 'Lord of Vaikuṇṭh,' an epithet of Vishṇu
vulg. baikunṭh-bāsī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Inhabiting paradise; deceased; — an inhabitant of paradise; a deceased person; — a celestial being.

def: s.m. pl. Name of a particular class of gods.
s.m. Affliction, misfortune, calamity; — punishment, torment; — reprisals, revenge
more: s. m. Calamity, reprisal, revenge. ويل کش wail-kash, adj. Vindictive.

def: adv. (dialec.) Near, hither, on this side.
s.m. Difference, disparity; opposition, contrariety.
s.m. Difference, disparity; opposition, contrariety.
s.m. Absence of mark or characteristic; — contrariety; reverse, inversion; — the reverse of what is usual or natural, unnaturalness, affectation.
conj. And if not, otherwise; — but, yet, still, however, nevertheless, notwithstanding; — unless.
more: adv. And if not (H. nahīṅ to ).

def: s.m. corr. of vairoćan, q.v. s.m. A patronymic of the son of Sūrya (the Sun); and of the son of Agni; and of the Daitya Bali.
adj. & s.m. = vaimānik, q.v. adj. Relating to a heavenly car; borne in divine chariots; — s.m. A god.
adj. Relating to a heavenly car; borne in divine chariots; — s.m. A god.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Born of a different mother; — a stepmother's son, a half-brother
s.m. (f. -ī) = vaimātra, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Born of a different mother; — a stepmother's son, a half-brother
s.m. Black grapes (rare).
s.m. A cutter of bamboos; a maker of bamboo work.
s.m. A name of Garuḍ (as son of Vinatā).
adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to a bamboo; produced from, or made of bamboo; — a bamboo-staff; — a worker in bamboo or wicker-work; — name of a sacred place of pilgrimage.
s.m. Flute-player, flutist, piper; — a lutist(?) (see vaiṇik).
v.t. To separate; to tear off, to flay, skin.
v.t. To separate; to tear off, to flay, skin.
s.m. A player on the Vīṇā or Indian lute, a lutist.
s.f. The wife of a baipārī, q.v.
s.m. Contrariety, opposition; adverseness; reverse (of); counterpart.
s.m. Heroism, valour, prowess; — enmity, hostility, &c. (i.q. bair, q.v.)
subs: (-se), To be inimical or hostile (to), to hate, &c. (For other compounds see bair.)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit वीर (vīra). Doublet of بیر (ber).

def: s.m. Anything forced upon a person; that which a person is forced to purchase.
s.m. = bairāg, q.v. s.m. Freedom from passion or from worldly attachments, subjection of the appetites; renunciation of worldly pleasures, seclusion from the world; devotion, austerity, penance
subs: To withdraw from the world, renounce the pleasures of the world; to lead the life of an ascetic; to become a recluse in consequence of unrequited love.

def: adj. & s.m. = bairāgī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Separated from passion or worldly attachments; austere, recluse; — one who has subdued his worldly desires and passions, one who abandons the pursuits of this world; ascetic, devotee, stoic, recluse; a class of Hindū faqīrs who roam the country and practise certain austerities.
s.f. = bairāgan, q.v. s.f. = H بيراگا बैरागा bairāgā, s.m. A small cross-shaped stick or piece of iron which a Bairāgī places under his arm-pit to lean upon as he sits.
s.m. = bairāg, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Heroic; — inimical, hostile, malicious, revengeful; — a hero; — an enemy, a foe, an adversary (i.q. bairī).
s.f. = vairiṇī, q.v. s.f. (see vairī) A heroine; — a female foe (i.q. bairan).
s.f. (see vairī) A heroine; — a female foe (i.q. bairan).
s.f. = S ويرتو वैरित्व vairitva, s.m. Heroism; — enmity, hostility, malice, hatred.
s.m. = S ويرتا वैरिता vairitā, s.f. Heroism; — enmity, hostility, malice, hatred.

ety: wair˚ prob. = S. व्यर्दय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+अर्द
def: v.t. (dialec.) To pour gradually from the hand or a vessel (rice, &c. into boiling water, grist into a mill, grain into a pan, or seed into the ground, &c.); to sow by drill; — to grind; — s.m. The vessel from which the grist is poured gradually into the mill the hopper; — a drill.
more: n. m. A drill vairnā ; Furrukh. ūirnā , v. a. 1. To sow by drill. 2. To grind. Chakkī vairnā .

def: s.m. A patronymic of the son of Sūrya (the Sun); and of the son of Agni; and of the Daitya Bali.
s.m. Difference or diversity of form or shape; (in Gram.) change of form; — deformity, malformation; ugliness.
s.f. = next, q.v.
s.m. = vaiśya, q.v. (and see bais).
more: s. m. 1. (for वैश्य ) A vaisya or man of the third, or agricultural and mercantile tribe. 2. (or vayas, for वयस् ) Age.

def: adj. A weak form of waisā, q.v.
s.m. contrac. of vaiśya, q.v.

def: adj. (f. -ī), That-like, such-like, such a, of that kind; — futile, vain, useless; aimless, objectless; — adv. Like that, in that manner, thus, so

adv. or adj. In that manner, so, that-like, such. ويسي کا ويسا waise kā waisā, Such as before.

s.m. = baisākh, q.v. s.m. The first of the twelve months constituting the Hindū solar year (answering to April-May).

adj. & s.f. = baisākhī, q.v. adj. & s.f. Of or relating to the month Baisākh; growing in Baisākh; — the day of full moon in the month of Baisākh; the crops of Baisākh; — a large kind of myrobalan or citron; — a club; a crutch ( = baisākhā).
adv. See s.v. waisā. adj. (f. -ī), That-like, such-like, such a, of that kind; — futile, vain, useless; aimless, objectless; — adv. Like that, in that manner, thus, so

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Characteristic; — belonging to the Vaiśeshik doctrine; — a follower of the Vaiśeshik doctrine; — (S. vaiśeshikam), name of one of the two great divisions of the Nyāya school of philosophy founded by Kaṇada.
= H ويسيشك वैसेशिक waiseśik, adj. & s.m. corr. of vaiśeshik, q.v.
= H ويسيسك वैसेसिक waisesik, adj. & s.m. corr. of vaiśeshik, q.v.
s.m. Inequality, unevenness, oddness; singleness; solitariness; — difficulty; calamity; misery, distress; injustice, harshness, rigour.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to an object of sense; sensual, carnal; — one occupied with worldly objects or interests, a worldly person, one addicted to the pleasures of sense, a sensualist.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to Vishṇu; worshipping Vishṇu; — a worshipper or follower of Vishṇu; name of one of the three great divisions of modern Hindū sects (the other two being the Śaivas and Śaktas)
more: 1. adj. Relating or belonging to Vish̤ṇu. 2. s. m. A sectary or follower of Vish̤ṇu.
subs: (˚va+ut˚), s.m. Lands granted rent-free to the worshippers of Vishṇu (esp. to those of the mendicant orders).

def: s.f. The personified Śakti or 'energy' of Vishṇu (regarded as one of the Mātr̤is); — a name of Durgā; — the plant Clitoria ternatea, and its flower; — sacred basil, Ocymum sanctum.
s.m. Name of a son of Viśvasan; — name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions.
s.m. = vaiśya = bais, qq.v.
s.f. A woman of the Vaiśya caste.
s.m. 'A man who occupies the soil, a man of the people,' a Vaiśya or man of the the third (Hindū) caste (whose business was agriculture and trade); — an agriculturist; a trader (see bais)
subs: s.m. or vaiśya-kriyā, s.f. The proper business or occupation of a Vaiśya, agriculture, trade.

ety: for A. وقفية, fem. of waqfī
def: s.f. The book wherein bequests for pious purposes are registered.
more: s. f. The book in which legacies for pious uses are registered.

def: s.m. = H وکهالنا वखालना wakhālnā, v.t. dialec. forms of ukhāl, and ukhālnā, qq.v.
v.t. = H وکهال वखाल wakhāl, s.m. dialec. forms of ukhāl, and ukhālnā, qq.v.
s. m. (dakh.) Trouble, in trouble: see تکليف taklīf.
adj. Informed, acquainted (with); aware (of), privy (to); — knowing, sensible, intelligent, versed (in), conversant (with), experienced, expert, skilful, learned
more: adj. وقف standing; stopping. Aware of; acquainted with; privy to (اُگاه). vāqif-kār, vāqif-hāl , n. m. 1. An experienced person. 2. A connoiseur. nā-vāqif , adj. 1. Unacquainted; unaware. 2. Ignorant; inexperienced.

(part. act. of وقف) adj. Acquainted, experienced, conversant, intelligent, knowing, learned, sensible. واقف حال يا کار wāḳif-i-ḥāl, or -kār, Experienced, intelligent, expert, a connoisseur. واقف کاري wāḳif-kār-ī, Experience, intelligence, expertness, knowledge.
subs: or wāqif-kār, adj. & s.m. Experienced; intelligent; expert, &c.; — an experienced person; an expert; a connoisseur
(-se, or -par), To become acquainted (with), or aware (of); to know, to understand
adj. Uninformed; unacquainted (with), unaware (of); — ignorant; inexperienced.
wety: From Arabic وَاقِف (wāqif).

ety: for A. واقفية, abst. s. fr. wāqif
def: s.f. Information, acquaintance, knowledge; experience; intelligence
more: n. f. Knowledge; privity (اَگاﮩن). vāqfiyat paidā k . To acquire a knowledge of, or acquaintance with. vāqfīyat jismānī . Carnal knowledge. vāqfiyat zātī . Personal knowledge.
s. f. Experience, knowledge, intelligence, acquaintance.
subs: (-se), To acquire a knowledge (of), or an acquaintance (with)

ety: v.n. fr. وکل 'to entrust (one's affairs, to another
def: s.m. An ambassador, agent, deputy, delegate, representative, commissary, factor, administrator; — an attorney, a pleader, counsellor (at law)
more: n. m. 1. An agent; an authorized pleader in a court of law. 2. An agent who represents an estate at the head quarters of another. vakīl sarkārī , n. m. A Government pleader. vakīl k. , v. a. To appoint a vakīl . vakīl mutlaq yā ām . 1. A plenipotentiary; a representative with absolute powers. 2. A permanently appointed pleader; a standing counsel.

(from وکل) s. m. An agent, ambassadour, representative, counsellor (at law), attorney, deputy, delegate, commissioner. وکيل مطلق wakīl-i-mut̤laḳ, A plenipotentiary, a vicegerent invested with full powers.
subs: v.t. To appoint (one) a wakīl; to declare one to be a representative (of)
s.m. A plenipotentiary; a vicegerent (or a representative) invested with full powers; — a permanently appointed pleader; standing counsel.

def: s.f. The office of a wakīl, &c. (i.q. wakālat, q.v.). s.f. The office of substitute, or agent, or administrator, &c.; substitute, proxy; agency; — embassy; deputation, delegation, commission; — the business of an advocate or pleader (wakīl), pleading, practice at the bar
subs: To act as substitute or proxy (for, -kī t̤araf-se); to advocate the cause (of); — to practise as a pleader or advocate
s.m. A plenipotentiary commission, full powers; — a power of attorney.

def: s.m. A weight of 2 ¼ lbs.; — wuqīya (for A. وقية wuqīyat), s.m. A weight of seven mis̤qāls and forty dirhams; an ounce.
more: s. A weight of seven mis̤ḳāl, an ounce.

def: s.m. Dignity, honour, reputation, character (i.q. waqār); — (prob. for A. waqur), adj. Mild; modest; — grave, sedate, staid
more: n. m. Dignity; honor. vaqar pānā v. n. 1. To obtain rank, dignity or influence. 2. To rise in one's esteem. vaqar khonā , v. n. 1. To lose one's character; disgrace oneself. 2. To make oneself cheap.
(Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Dignity, honour, character. adj. Mild, modest, sedate.
subs: To obtain dignity, or rank, or influence; — to rise in the esteem (of)
(apnā), To lose (one's) reputation or character, to disgrace ooneself; — to lower one's dignity, to make oneself cheap.

ety: or wāṅkṛā; Prk. वंकडओ; S. वंक+र+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) An ornament (of gold or silver) for the arm (esp. of a child); — a kind of anklet.

adj. Crooked, curved, bent, bowed; winding, meandering; tortuous; — retrograde (motion of a planet); — crooked in disposition; cunning; fraudulent, dishonest; prevaricating; indirect, evasive, ambiguous; — cruel, malignant; — s.m. The planet Mars; the planet Saturn; — an epithet of Rudra or Śiva; — (S. vakram) winding course (of a river); arm or bend (of a stream); — apparent retrograde motion (of a planet)
subs: s.m. The tree Sesbana grandiflora; and Butea frondosa
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Going crookedly, moving tortuously or in zigzag; tortuous; fraudulent, dishonest; evasive; — a fraudulent man, &c.
s.f. Crooked or winding course, tortuous motion; retrograde motion, or retrogression (of a planet); — foppishness, airs
s.m. Curvature; curve; fraudulent or dishonest disposition
(˚ra+uk˚), s.f. Indirect or perverse speech, equivocation, prevarication, evasive speech or reply, evasion; an equivoque, a double-entendre; hint, insinuation; pun; sarcasm.

def: s.f. Crookedness; curvature; tortuousness; — retrograde motion (of a planet); — the going crooked or wrong; failure, mishap; — perverseness; falseness, dishonesty, fraudulence; — evasive or indirect speech, prevarication; ambiguity.
adj. (f. -iṇī), Crooked, curved, bent; tortuous; — moving backwards, retrograding (as a planet); — dishonest, fradulent.
s.m. See s.v. vakr. s.m. Dignity, honour, reputation, character (i.q. waqār); — (prob. for A. waqur), adj. Mild; modest; — grave, sedate, staid
subs: To obtain dignity, or rank, or influence; — to rise in the esteem (of)
(apnā), To lose (one's) reputation or character, to disgrace ooneself; — to lower one's dignity, to make oneself cheap.

def: s.m. The breast, bosom, chest
subs: vulg. vakshoj, or vaksho-ruh, s.m. The female breast (lit. 'chest-born,' or 'chest-growing')
or vakshah-sthal, s.m. 'Place of the breast,' the breast, bosom; the female breast; the heart.

def: s.m. Time; term; fixed time (for); season; hour; — duration; juncture; — opportunity; — (met.) adversity, distress
more: n. m. pl. auqāt . See belā , (1). 1. Time; term. 1. Vaqt kī khūbī hai . 2. Vaqt vaqt kī rāgni hai . Prov. Every rāgnī (song) has its appropriate hour and season. 3. Vaqt ko ganīmar samjho! Prov. Consider the present time sufficient. (Make the best of your time). 4. Donoṅ vaqt mile nahīṅ sīte, Sūraj kī āṅkh phūṭ jāëgī! Wom. One should not sew when day meets night, the eye of day would be put out! 5. Mehr-bān ho-ke bulā lo mujhe chāho jis vaqt, Maiṅ gayā-vaqt nahīṅ hūṅ ki phir ā bhī na sakūṅ . Gālib. To call me back whene'er you wish do you but deign, I'm not as time that's past which cannot come again. 2. Duration. 3. Opportunity. 4. Adversity. Vaqt to paigambaroṅ par bhī paṛtā hai. vaqt be-mauqah, vaqt nā-sāz . An unseasonable juncture. vaqt be-vaqt . 1. In season and out of season. 2. At all times. 3. Sometimes. vaqt par , adv. At the proper time; seasonably; in the nick of time; in due…

s. m. Time, season, opportunity. ( met. ) Adversity. Plur. اوقات auḳāt, s. f. Times, &c. وقت بوقت waḳt ba-waḳt, From time to time. وقت بيوقت waḳt be-waḳt, In season or out of season, a time that admits not of delay, constantly, perpetually. وقت پر waḳt par, At (proper) time, seasonably, betimes. وقت پڙنا waḳt paṛnā, To suffer misfortunes, to be distressed. وقت کاٿنا waḳt kāṭnā, To pass away the time. وقت کي وقت waḳt ke waḳt, At all times. وقت نازک waḳt-i-nāzuk, Delicate times. وقت نا وقت waḳt nā waḳt, Occasionally, without any stated time. وقت ياب waḳt-yāb, Finding an opportunity.
subs: or waql barābar ā-jānā (-kā), The days (of a person), to be numbered
adv. From time to time; — now and again
s.m. An inopportune moment; unseasonable juncture
adv. In season and out of season, at all times, constantly, perpetually; — a time that admits not of delay; — sometimes
adv. At the proper time, seasonably; — in due course; — at the opportune moment, in the nick of time; in time of need
v.n. Occasion to arise (for); need (for a thing) to arise; to stand in need (of); adversity or misfortune to befall (-par), to suffer misfortune, to be in distress
adv. On occasion arising; — on misfortune befalling, in time of adversity
or waqt dekhnā, To watch an opportunity
adj. 'Observant of the proper time,' punctual
To pass away, or to beguile, the time
s.f. A thing suited to the need of the hour; — a song suited to the particular hour of the day or night
adv. At the very moment, at the nick of time; — at all times
To pass time; to kill time
s.m. The fixed or appointed time, the hour of death
s.m. Unsuitable or inconvenient time; an unseasonable juncture
adv. At irregular hours, unseasonably; occasionally; without any stated time
v.n. Time to pass or to elapse; an opportunity to pass away
To lose no time or opportunity
(-ě), adv. In or at the time (of); while
adj. & adv. Out of time or season, untimely, unseasonable; ill-timed, at the wrong time or moment; premature
adv. At the very nick of time
adv. During leisure hours; after time
adv. At what time, at the time that; whenever
adv. Till such time as; as long as
(waqtaṉ, Ar. acc. of waqt), adv. From time to time.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -trī), Speaking, talking; talkative, loquacious; — speaking well or sensibly, eloquent; learned, wise; — a speaker, talker; — a loquacious man; — an eloquent man; a learned or wise man, a teacher.
adv. From time to time.

part. adj. (f. -ā), Fit to be spoken or uttered, fit or proper to be said; to be named or called; to be spoken to or addressed; — to be spoken about or against, having a bad name or reputation, objectionable, reprehensible, vile, low, base, bad; — liable to be called upon for an account, accountable, answerable, responsible; — subject, dependent; — s.m. Speaking; speech; a rule, precept, sentence, dictum, aphorism; — a subject treated of; the meaning (of a passage).
adj. Of or relating to a certain time or season; timely, seasonable.
In the nick of time.
(dakh.) See وقت پر waḳt-par.
